name: Poblado de la Almoyola — gravel loop from Pliego
name_original: Poblado de la Almoyola — vuelta en gravel desde Pliego
region_intro: Touring cycling around Pliego offers diverse terrain, from verdant river valleys to the rugged foothills of Sierra Espuña. The region features a blend of paved and unpaved segments, with routes that include significant elevation gains. Cyclists can explore areas characterized by dramatic landscapes, historical sites, and the deep marks carved by the Pliego and Mula rivers. This varied topography provides options for different skill levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Pliego The most popular touring cycling route is Poblado de la Almoyola — gravel loop from Pliego, a 17.4 miles (28.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate gravel loop offers a scenic ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route incorporates an impressive viaduct over the Mula River and views of La Luz Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Mula River Viaduct – Salto del Usero loop from La Almoloya, a 41.0 miles (66.0 km) trail leading through the Mula River valley and past Salto del Usero, often completed in about 5 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Pliego is defined by the rugged topography of Sierra Espuña, the green river valleys of the Mula and Pliego rivers, and routes that pass historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Pliego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 65 reviews. More than 520 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pliego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Pliego?
A: Pliego offers nearly 50 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 23 moderate and 23 difficult routes, plus a couple of easier options, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Pliego?
A: Touring cycling around Pliego features a diverse landscape, from the verdant river valleys of the Mula and Pliego rivers to the rugged foothills of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. You can expect a mix of paved and unpaved segments, with routes offering significant elevation gains, especially as you approach the mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Pliego?
A: Yes, many routes around Pliego are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017215355/poblado-de-la-almoyola-gravel-loop-from-pliego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado de la Almoyola — gravel loop from Pliego&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic circular ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Pliego's routes often pass by significant natural and historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5533456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, explore the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3016870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Baños de Mula Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Viaduct – Salto del Usero loop from La Almoloya&lt;/a&gt; even takes you past the beautiful Salto del Usero waterfall.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: While many routes in Pliego are moderate to difficult, there are a couple of easier options available. For a moderate gravel ride that's manageable, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017215355/poblado-de-la-almoyola-gravel-loop-from-pliego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado de la Almoyola — gravel loop from Pliego&lt;/a&gt;. When planning, look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Pliego?
A: The Region of Murcia generally enjoys a mild climate, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling in Pliego. During these seasons, temperatures are pleasant, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, so plan accordingly.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Pliego?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Pliego, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 65 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty and historical sites, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents, especially those offering views of Sierra Espuña.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Sierra Espuña Regional Park?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Pliego either pass through or offer stunning views of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, known for its rugged topography and extensive pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14626415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through pine trees – Sierra Espuña North-facing Slope loop from La Almoloya&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you directly into this beautiful natural area.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Pliego?
A: Many routes originate from towns like Pliego or Mula, where public parking is generally available. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as parking information is often provided by other cyclists who have completed the tour.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or significant water bodies?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Viaduct – Salto del Usero loop from La Almoloya&lt;/a&gt; will take you past Salto del Usero, a beautiful natural space near Bullas featuring an impressive waterfall and turquoise pool. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Luz Reservoir – Mula River Viaduct loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt; offer views of the La Luz Reservoir.

Q: Can I find routes that include historical sites or archaeological remains?
A: Absolutely. Pliego and its surroundings are rich in history. Routes often incorporate historical points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3016870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Baños de Mula Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts ancient castles like the Castle of Pliego and archaeological remains from the Argar period, which can be explored on routes passing through areas like Almoloya.
tips: The Clock Tower is a 19th century building located in the town of Pliego and on the clock street, which gives the hours of the day announcing them with the only bell that crowns the tower. It consists of three spheres that give sight to the entire town.

Located in the municipality of Pliego, the Poblado de la Almoyola is an archaeological site of great importance. La Almoloya is an archaeological site belonging to the Argaric Culture. The site is known to have been occupied between 2200 and 1550 BC. and discovered in 1944.
The discoveries have allowed us to conclude that La Almoloya was a political and administrative center of the territory of El Argar.
Nice and quiet road near the town of Almoyola to take a bike ride near the municipality of Pliego.
